<!--
Thank you for your contribution.
This template is intended for well-structured and reasoned pull requests.
Please ensure your writing is clear and precise, as others will refer to this PR for understanding the decision-making process.
You may remove any sections that are not relevant.
-->

# Preface

<!--
Start with the “why.” What motivated this change? Was it technical debt, an upstream issue, a tooling failure, or a shift in priorities?

Explain how you arrived at this decision. Personal observations, relevant links to issues, forums, or commits are welcome.
-->

## Description of Change

<!--
What does this PR *do* in concrete terms?

Describe the changes in sufficient technical detail for another contributor (or future-you) to follow the logic.
List specific replacements, removals, or rewrites — especially any changes that affect how the project is built, run, or structured.
-->

## Possible Alternatives

<!--
What other approaches did you consider before settling on this one?

If you evaluated other tools, rewrites, or ideas (and decided against them), explain why.
This helps future contributors understand the tradeoffs.
-->

## Implementation Details

<!--
Elaborate on parts that may not be obvious from the code diff.

If you adopted a nonstandard pattern or chose not to use common tooling, explain why.
-->

## Notes

<!--
Call out anything that isn’t final yet or that requires a follow-up.

This could include TODOs, known limitations, partial rewrites, or behavioral changes that need documentation/user attention.
Include related issues, discussions, RFCs, or historical pull requests that provide context.
-->

<!--
- Fixes: #
- Supersedes: #
- Related to: #
-->


## Tested on

<!--
If this PR introduces functional changes (new behavior, fixed bugs, UI, CLI output, etc.), indicate which OSes you tested it on.
-->

- [ ] Windows — Version: `N/A`
- [ ] Linux — Distro & Version: `N/A`
- [ ] macOS — Version: `N/A`
- [ ] FreeBSD (optional) — Details: `N/A`
